question.text: Which was the first commodity to have organized derivatives trading in India?

explanation.text: **The Logic:** Organized trading in commodity derivatives started in India in 1875 by the Bombay Cotton Trade Association Limited, with cotton as the underlying commodity. **The Trap:** Rice was the first globally (Osaka, 1730). Jute trading started in 1919 (Calcutta Hessian Exchange). Castor seed was traded by Guajrati Vyapari Mandali, which came after Bombay Cotton Trade Association. **Cross-Exam Context:** Series XII (Securities Markets Foundation) covers evolution of Indian capital markets. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** India has one of the oldest commodity derivatives markets — this heritage now supports a sophisticated multi-exchange electronic trading ecosystem.
